critical.mandate: User-initiated cross-sidecar reads are explicitly allowed per agent-specification-protocol
Never modify a sidecar file without explicit user confirmation for each entry
Preserve the sidecar file header and marker comment in all modifications
Process sidecars one at a time — release previous sidecar content before loading next (JIT)
Archival is recommendation-only — never auto-execute without user confirmation
Cross-reference validation scope is limited to the declared matrix in _memory/config.yaml
Token approximation: 4 chars per token (file size in bytes / 4)

step.title: Dynamic Sidecar Discovery
step.action: Read {memory_path}/config.yaml to get tier assignments, token budgets, and cross-reference matrix
Extract tier_1, tier_2, tier_3 agent lists from config. For each agent, resolve sidecar directory name from the agents.{agent-id}.sidecar field
Enumerate all on-disk {memory_path}/*-sidecar/ directories

Union config-declared sidecars and on-disk sidecars into a master list. For each sidecar, classify by tier: - If agent is in tiers.tier_1.agents → Tier 1 (3 files: ground-truth.md, decision-log.md, conversation-context.md) - If agent is in tiers.tier_2.agents → Tier 2 (2 files: decision-log.md, conversation-context.md) - If agent is in tiers.tier_3.agents → Tier 3 (1 file: decision-log.md) - If agent has on-disk sidecar but no tier in config → classify as Untiered (scan decision-log.md only, no budget enforced)
Exclude archive/ subdirectories from active scanning — these contain archived entries and are gitignored

Build categorized sidecar lists: - Tier 1 sidecars with expected files - Tier 2 sidecars with expected files - Tier 3 sidecars with expected files - Untiered sidecars (decision-log.md only) - Empty sidecars (no content files found) - Sidecars with legacy filenames (architecture-decisions.md, infrastructure-decisions.md, threat-model-decisions.md, velocity-data.md) — flag for migration to standard names
step.check: Report "All sidecars are empty — nothing to review" and complete the workflow
step.action: Report to user: "{N} sidecars discovered ({T1} Tier 1, {T2} Tier 2, {T3} Tier 3, {U} untiered), {E} empty. {L} legacy filenames detected. Proceeding with review."

step.title: Tier-Aware Multi-File Scanning
step.action: For each sidecar (JIT — release previous sidecar content before loading next): - Determine expected files based on tier classification from Step 1 - Tier 1: read ground-truth.md, decision-log.md, conversation-context.md - Tier 2: read decision-log.md, conversation-context.md - Tier 3: read decision-log.md - Untiered: read decision-log.md

For each expected file: - Check if file exists - If missing: log warning "Expected {filename} for {tier} agent {agent} — file not found" - If exists: check for content beyond template header (text after marker comment) - Record file size in bytes for token budget calculation

Detect legacy filenames in each sidecar: - architecture-decisions.md → should be decision-log.md (architect-sidecar) - infrastructure-decisions.md → should be decision-log.md (devops-sidecar) - threat-model-decisions.md → should be decision-log.md (security-sidecar) - velocity-data.md → should be decision-log.md (sm-sidecar) If legacy file found: scan it as decision-log content but flag for migration
Build content inventory: list of all files scanned with size, tier, and content status (has-content / empty / missing / legacy-name)

step.title: Load Reference Artifacts
step.action: Attempt to read each reference artifact (record which exist and which are missing): - {planning_artifacts}/architecture.md — for architectural decision validation - {planning_artifacts}/prd.md — for product decision validation - {planning_artifacts}/infrastructure-design.md — for infrastructure validation - {test_artifacts}/test-plan.md — for test strategy validation - {implementation_artifacts}/sprint-status.yaml — for sprint references and current sprint ID - {planning_artifacts}/epics-and-stories.md — for story/epic key validation
For each artifact that exists: retain its key sections and structure for comparison
Extract current sprint ID from sprint-status.yaml if it exists. If not found, note "sprint data unavailable — will use 42-day calendar fallback for age calculations"
step.check: Warn user: "No reference artifacts found. Hygiene will be limited to structural checks and budget reporting."

step.title: Cross-Reference Validation
step.action: For each sidecar with content (JIT — release previous before loading next): Parse decision-log entries using the standardized format (E9-S3): - Header: ### [YYYY-MM-DD] Decision Title - Fields: Agent, Sprint, Status: active | superseded | archived, Related: {paths, keys} - If entry does not follow standardized format (no Status field, no ### header): flag as UNVERIFIABLE-FORMAT

For entries with Status field: - If Status: superseded or Status: archived → flag entry itself as already-archived (candidate for archive/ move) - If Status: active → validate Related field references

For entries with Related field pointing to other agents' decisions: - Use the cross-reference matrix from {memory_path}/config.yaml to determine valid cross-agent reads - For each cross-agent reference: look up the referenced entry's Status in the source agent's decision-log - If referenced entry has Status: superseded or archived → flag as STALE with evidence - If referenced entry not found → flag as ORPHANED - If source agent's file not parseable → flag reference as UNVERIFIABLE-FORMAT

For all entries: scan Related field for artifact paths and story/epic keys - Cross-reference paths against filesystem — if not found, flag as potential STALE or ORPHANED - Cross-reference story/epic keys against epics-and-stories.md — if not found, flag as ORPHANED - For ORPHANED candidates: search for semantically similar names and suggest likely renames

step.title: Stale Detection via Skill
step.action: JIT-load the stale-detection section from {project-root}/_gaia/lifecycle/skills/memory-management.md Load only the content between <!-- SECTION: stale-detection --> and <!-- END SECTION --> markers

Apply the canonical stale detection logic from the memory-management skill: 1. Stale entries — decision references an artifact path that no longer exists on the filesystem 2. Contradicted entries — two active decisions in the same sidecar that conflict 3. Orphaned entries — decision references a story or epic removed from epics-and-stories.md
Merge stale detection results with cross-reference validation results from Step 4 — deduplicate entries flagged by both checks

step.title: Classify Entries
step.action: Assign each sidecar entry one of five statuses: - ACTIVE: Entry is consistent with current reference artifacts — no action needed - STALE: The referenced artifact has changed or the cross-referenced decision is superseded/archived - CONTRADICTED: Current artifact explicitly states the opposite of what this entry records - ORPHANED: The component, service, story, or epic referenced no longer exists in any current artifact - UNVERIFIABLE-FORMAT: Entry does not follow standardized format (no Status field) — cannot validate programmatically
Record evidence for each classification: which artifact section confirms or contradicts, what changed, or what is missing

step.title: Token Budget Reporting
step.action: For each agent sidecar, calculate token usage: - Sum file sizes in bytes across all sidecar files (from Step 2 inventory) - Convert to approximate token count: file size in bytes / 4 (chars-per-token approximation from config archival.token_approximation) - For Tier 1 agents: also calculate ground-truth.md token usage separately

Compare against budgets from {memory_path}/config.yaml: - Tier 1 session budget: tiers.tier_1.session_budget (300K tokens) - Tier 1 ground truth budget: agents.{agent}.ground_truth_budget (per-agent: Val 200K, Theo 150K, Derek 100K, Nate 100K) - Tier 2 session budget: tiers.tier_2.session_budget (100K tokens) - Tier 3: no budget enforced - Untiered: no budget enforced
Apply threshold status indicators per archival config: - OK: below 80% of budget - warning: at or above 80% but below 90% - critical: at or above 90% but below 100% - over-budget: at or above 100%

Build Token Budget Table with columns: | Agent | Tier | Files Scanned | Token Usage | Session Budget | GT Budget | % Used | Status | For Tier 3 and untiered agents: budget column shows "no budget enforced" with actual token count reported

step.title: Archival Recommendations
step.action: Generate archival recommendations based on three criteria: (a) Budget pressure — for agents at or above 90% of budget: identify oldest entries as archival candidates (oldest first). Mark as "actionable — budget pressure" (b) Staleness — entries with Status: superseded or Status: archived that have not been moved to the archive/ subdirectory. Mark as "actionable — stale status" (c) Age — entries older than 3 sprints (determined from current sprint ID minus 3; fallback to 42 calendar days if no sprint data). Only flag if the entry has no Related field pointing to an active story. Mark as "advisory — age"
Classify each recommendation: - Actionable: budget pressure and staleness recommendations — should be acted on - Advisory: age-based recommendations — informational, user decides
Archival is never auto-executed — all recommendations require user confirmation per entry before any file changes are made

step.title: Ground Truth Refresh Trigger
step.action: For each Tier 1 agent (Val, Theo, Derek, Nate) that has a ground-truth.md file: - Read the most recent entry's Sprint field from ground-truth.md - Compare against current sprint ID from sprint-status.yaml - If the gap is more than 1 sprint behind: recommend /gaia-refresh-ground-truth for that agent
If sprint-status.yaml does not exist or has no sprint ID: skip ground truth refresh check (cannot determine staleness without sprint data)

If E9-S11 (--agent parameter) is not yet implemented: recommend base command /gaia-refresh-ground-truth without --agent flag. Once E9-S11 ships, recommend /gaia-refresh-ground-truth --agent {agent-id}
Tier 2 and Tier 3 agents have no ground-truth.md — exclude from this check entirely

step.title: Present Enhanced Report
step.action:
Generate the enhanced findings report with these 7 sections:
**1. Summary** - Total sidecars scanned (by tier: Tier 1, Tier 2, Tier 3, untiered) - Total entries scanned - Counts by status: ACTIVE, STALE, CONTRADICTED, ORPHANED, UNVERIFIABLE-FORMAT - Budget usage summary: agents OK, warning, critical, over-budget
**2. Token Budget Table** | Agent | Tier | Files Scanned | Token Usage | Session Budget | GT Budget | % Used | Status | (One row per agent, sorted by tier then agent name)
**3. Detailed Findings** | Sidecar | Entry | Status | Evidence | Reference Artifact | (Grouped by sidecar, sorted by severity: CONTRADICTED, STALE, ORPHANED, UNVERIFIABLE-FORMAT, ACTIVE)
**4. Archival Recommendations** | # | Agent | Entry | Reason | Type | Action | (Budget pressure first, then staleness, then age)
**5. Ground Truth Refresh Recommendations** | Agent | Last Sprint | Current Sprint | Gap | Recommendation | (Tier 1 agents only)
**6. Untiered Agent Report** | Agent | Sidecar Dir | Files Found | Recommendation | For each untiered agent: recommend adding to _memory/config.yaml as Tier 3
**7. Skipped Sidecars** List of sidecars with no content or explicitly skipped
step.template-output: Enhanced memory hygiene report with all 7 sections: Summary, Token Budget Table, Detailed Findings, Archival Recommendations, Ground Truth Refresh Recommendations, Untiered Agent Report, Skipped Sidecars.

step.title: User Action on Flagged Items
step.action: For each entry with status other than ACTIVE, ask the user to choose one action: - Keep: Add a [Reviewed: {date}] annotation to the entry — confirms user saw and accepted it - Archive: Move the entry to the archive/ subdirectory of the sidecar (if directory exists; create if not). Prefix with [Archived: {date} — Reason: {status}] - Delete: Remove the entry from the sidecar file entirely

When modifying sidecar files: - ALWAYS preserve the file header (title, description, marker comment) - ALWAYS keep the <!-- Decisions will be appended below this line --> marker intact - Active entries remain between the marker comment and end of file - Archived entries go to archive/ subdirectory, not inline section
After all user actions are processed: report summary of changes made to each sidecar file

step.title: Optional: Prune Completed Checkpoints
step.ask: Would you like to prune old completed checkpoints? (yes / skip)
step.action: Read {checkpoint_path}/completed/ and list all .yaml files with dates
step.ask: How many sprints of checkpoints to retain? (default: 2)
step.action: Delete checkpoint files older than the retention window
Report: "Pruned {N} completed checkpoint(s). Retained {M} within the {retention} sprint window."
